title: Static-key mutual auth (Noise-XX) + bind NodeId to static key
rationale: Handshake is unauthenticated ephemeral-ephemeral DH (Noise NN): complete() trusts ANY peer key, so MITM/Sybil is indistinguishable from a real peer. Single largest crypto gap; every downstream AEAD/replay/routing defense currently protects a channel to an unauthenticated stranger. libp2p runs Noise-XX over our exact X25519+ChaCha20-Poly1305 primitives. Foundation for B02/B03/B04.
board_module: trios-mesh crypto (src/crypto.rs) - M1, tri-net#10

title: Gate ETX/routing on completed auth; MAC/sign HELLOs (kill Sybil + blackhole)
rationale: Plaintext HELLOs let an attacker forge src and lie in the 'heard' list to drive its ETX->1.0, become best_next_hop(), then blackhole traffic (classic AODV false-metric attack). Only enter a neighbor into EtxTable after an authenticated handshake proves it holds the static key; one static key = one identity collapses Sybil and rate-limits HELLO floods that drain the endurance-limited node.
board_module: trios-mesh routing+discovery (src/routing.rs, src/discovery.rs) - M2, tri-net#11

title: Fast failure detection via HELLO seq gaps (BFD-style) -> ETX=INF, recompute
rationale: Hello already carries seq but daemon.rs ignores it. After k consecutive missed beacons force EtxTable to mark the link INFINITY and trigger recompute instead of waiting for the averaging window to decay. Bounds self-heal convergence to k*beacon_interval, giving the DEMO GATE a concrete testable number and directly attacking the 'self-healing threshold UNDEFINED' greenfield risk.
board_module: trios-mesh discovery+daemon (src/discovery.rs, src/daemon.rs) - P2 DEMO GATE
citation: BFD RFC 5880; Cisco/Arista hello-multiplier fast-failover guidance

title: Beacon scheduler + neighbor-expiry sweep in the daemon
rationale: daemon.rs has no HELLO scheduling and no neighbor timeout: record() only fires on received HELLOs, so a dead neighbor never accumulates misses and never decays, and a silent neighbor lingers forever. Add a periodic beacon tick and an expiry sweep that record()s a miss per interval. Prerequisite for both the EWMA (B05) and seq-gap (B03) items to behave correctly.
board_module: trios-mesh daemon (src/daemon.rs) - P1 radio+mesh

title: Replace flat-window delivery ratio with EWMA/WMEWMA + optimistic bootstrap
rationale: DeliveryRatio is a uniform sliding-window mean - the exact slow-reacting form: at ~12 m/s UAV cruise, topology changes faster than the averaging window, so ETX is stale exactly when it matters. WMEWMA (alpha~0.3-0.5) stays responsive yet filters PRR noise. Also bootstrap fresh neighbors with an optimistic prior instead of returning 0.0 => INFINITY. Drop-in replacement of ratio(), keep record()/window API. Closes the top mobility weakness.
board_module: trios-mesh routing metric (src/routing.rs DeliveryRatio) - P1 radio+mesh
citation: Rosati et al. arXiv:1307.6350; WMEWMA (Woo & Culler); arXiv:1311.3746

title: Multi-hop additive path ETX + Babel-style feasibility condition
rationale: best_next_hop() ranks only DIRECT neighbors: no multi-hop path metric and no loop-avoidance, in a 3-8 node relay mesh whose whole point is relaying. Real FANET flights show Babel (feasibility-condition distance-vector + sequenced updates) beats OLSR and BATMAN-adv. Implement additive path ETX with per-source seq and a feasibility check (accept only if strictly better/fresher) for cheap loop-freedom. Host-testable in -sim.
board_module: trios-mesh routing table (src/routing.rs) - P1 radio+mesh

title: Host-side single-carrier + OFDM modem sim to fix MCS/link-budget before RTL
rationale: Before committing PL resources, a host DSP model quantifies BER-vs-SNR for BPSK/QPSK/16/64-QAM with conv (and optional LDPC) FEC over AWGN+mild-Ricean, produces AMC SNR-switching thresholds, fixes FFT size/CP length/MCS table, and validates the 5.8 GHz link budget incl. 4-7 dB OFDM PAPR back-off vs low-PAPR DFT-spread. Pure software, testable like the crypto core; de-risks all downstream RTL and lets us numerically compare OFDM vs SC for the open-sky channel.
board_module: Host / trios-mesh sim (-sim), no hardware

title: GF16 Rust DSP model of the OFDM FFT/equalizer datapath (sim-first)
rationale: GF16 is silicon-proven at 323 MHz on Artix-7 (same family as AX7203) with a ready Rust FFI (goldenfloat-sys) and phi_dot/phi_fma ops. A gf16 datapath module gives a bit-exact 16-bit reference model of the FFT/equalizer taps that maps 1:1 to t27-emitted Verilog, attacking the top greenfield risk (demod fit in 85K-LC/220-DSP): half the multiplier width vs fp32 => more parallel taps per DSP48. Realistic gain is area/width, NOT accuracy (paper makes no superiority claim). Validate against ml_dtypes-cross-checked conformance vectors, not asserted.
board_module: P201/P203 Mini radio PL datapath; trios-mesh src/ new gf16 module
citation: arXiv:2606.05017 (GF16 323MHz XC7A35T 35/35); gh:gHashTag/zig-golden-float rust/goldenfloat-sys phi_dot C-ABI

title: Authenticate HELLO beacons + freshness (seq+timestamp under MAC)
rationale: FrameKind::Hello exists in wire.rs but HELLOs bypass AEAD entirely and carry only a plaintext u32 seq with no replay/freshness check, so old HELLOs can be replayed to resurrect dead links or skew ETX history. Run HELLOs through the authenticated session (or a broadcast MAC keyed off the static identity) and enforce monotonic seq + timestamp window per neighbor. Extends the existing 64-frame DATA replay defense to the control plane. Gated on B01.
board_module: trios-mesh discovery+wire (src/discovery.rs, src/wire.rs)
citation: SAODV non-mutable-field signing sciencedirect S1084804512000331; libsodium replay-window

title: Periodic rekey / HKDF ratchet + zeroize all key material
rationale: Session uses one HKDF key for its whole lifetime with a monotonic u64 counter and never rekeys; a high-throughput long session approaches ChaCha20's 2^32-block (256 GB) per-key limit, and a captured flying node leaks a long-lived key. Add an HKDF-chained ratchet every N frames/T seconds (or fresh-ephemeral re-handshake) with a hard cap before any per-key data/nonce limit, and wrap key material (HKDF output, EphemeralSecret, session key) in zeroize so it is wiped on rekey/drop. Both purely additive Rust.
board_module: trios-mesh crypto (src/crypto.rs) - M1, tri-net#10

title: Define + instrument M5 self-heal convergence pass/fail metric
rationale: Convergence threshold is undefined (greenfield risk). Adopt roadmap draft <5 s link-loss re-route / <10 s node-off recovery as the GO/NO-GO metric, grounded in Babel's fast reconvergence and the self-healing topology literature, and instrument convergence timing on the existing ETX metric in routing.rs/daemon.rs (bounded via B03 to k*beacon_interval). Converts an undefined risk into a measurable gate; low effort, defensible for the pitch.
board_module: trios-mesh routing+daemon (src/routing.rs, src/daemon.rs) - P2 DEMO GATE

title: Node-disjoint backup next-hop for instant failover + load spread
rationale: The triangle gives every node two potential next hops but best_next_hop() returns only one, so a link break stalls until reconvergence. Keep the second-best node-disjoint next hop as a hot backup (LB-OPAR: up to +30% flow success, +4x throughput from load-balanced near-disjoint paths) and switch instantly on the B03 dead signal. O(|E|^2) trivial at 8 nodes. True source-routed multipath needs a path field added to the 11-byte wire header (bumps VERSION).
board_module: trios-mesh routing (src/routing.rs) + wire (src/wire.rs) - P2 DEMO GATE
citation: Sharma et al. LB-OPAR arXiv:2205.07126; interference-aware multipath IEEE 4428723

title: Ternary (BitNet b1.58) RF-interference/jam classifier stub + ETX jam hook
rationale: BitNet b1.58 ternary MLP exists in Gamma RTL (bitnet_encoder.v) and is add-only (R-SI-1), so ternary inference needs no DSP48 and can run alongside the OFDM datapath on the DSP-starved 7020. Ship a Rust ternary classifier stub (add-only forward pass) that consumes AD9361 spectral/RSSI/LQI features and outputs jam/clear + suggested channel/TX-power, feeding a 'link jammed' flag into EtxTable so best_next_hop() re-routes around a jammed hop instead of thrashing. Mitigates jamming + weak-PA range via smarter control, not watts. Gains qualitative until trained on real captures - label [Conj].
board_module: P201/P203 Mini onboard AI + trios-mesh routing (src/routing.rs) + SDR driver iface

title: Adopt 84-format conformance vectors + 0x47C0 anchor as datapath golden CI tests
rationale: The 84-format catalog ships SHA-256-fingerprinted, ml_dtypes-cross-validated bit-exact vectors, and the mechanical anchor 3.0=phi^2+phi^-2 / dot4(1,2,3,4)=0x47C0 recurs across all four tt-trinity dice. Importing these as golden vectors into the GF16 datapath (B08) CI gives a single source-of-truth so the datapath is bit-identical in -sim and on the Zynq PL, de-risking the 'never run on hardware' gap. Low effort, high verification leverage, no perf claim.
board_module: trios-mesh CI + gf16 module tests; ref gh:gHashTag/t27 FORMAT-SPEC-001.json, tt-trinity-corona

title: Multi-uplink ETX-weighted default-route failover (M4)
rationale: Single uplink point (MED) dissolves via dual-homing: a second standby uplink (4G backup already planned) on a different triangle node with ETX-weighted default-route failover means the mesh survives loss of the primary uplink. Pure software in the daemon's NAT/route-propagation (M4), no new hardware beyond a second modem. Triangle already provides the path redundancy.
board_module: trios-mesh daemon (src/daemon.rs) - M4 shared-uplink NAT

title: BLAKE3 tamper-evident audit log of C2/telemetry frames (Rust now, FPGA offload later)
rationale: Euler/Gamma silicon RTL already implements blake3_anchor.v + audit_log_ring_buffer.v + proof_trace_writer.v; trios-mesh M1 does ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD + HKDF-SHA256 in crypto.rs/wire.rs. Add a BLAKE3-hashed append-only audit log of C2/telemetry frames (Rust now) to address the injection weak spot and produce an auditable command trail strengthening the BVLOS/CAAS regulatory case. Gain is integrity offload + audit, not new crypto strength (AEAD already gives confidentiality/authenticity). HW BLAKE3 offload is future silicon.
board_module: trios-mesh crypto/wire (src/crypto.rs, src/wire.rs); Zynq PL BLAKE3 offload future

title: Rate-limit unknown-src frames before allocation + widen/parametrize replay window
rationale: Two low-effort hardenings: (1) daemon.rs open_data looks up session by CLAIMED src, so an attacker can spam arbitrary-src frames to force lookups/table growth (cheap DoS on the CPU/power-limited node) - add a pre-auth cheap-reject path with a bounded neighbor table and per-src rate limiting before any allocation; (2) the fixed 64-frame ReplayWindow can false-reject legitimately reordered frames on the lossy multi-hop path (~50% half-duplex increases reordering) - widen to a 128/256-bit bitmap or make WIDTH configurable, with a heavy-reorder test.
board_module: trios-mesh daemon+crypto (src/daemon.rs, src/crypto.rs)
citation: arxiv.org/pdf/1407.3987; researchgate 308541512; crypto.rs ReplayWindow WIDTH=64; libsodium

title: Keep sim/demo topology a triangle (not a chain) + constant-time CI audit
rationale: Two small assurance items: (1) set the default -sim topology and physical anchor placement to a triangle (FANET half-duplex results favor it; it's the precondition for B12's 2 next-hop candidates), placing the anchor near the Galkin optimal-height regime; (2) add a CI/build check that RustCrypto backends compile to portable constant-time code on armv7 (ChaCha20 is naturally constant-time; Cortex-A9 has no AES-NI) and forbid any early-return-on-tag-mismatch. Config/test only, no product-code risk.
board_module: trios-mesh tests/smoke harness + CI (.github)
citation: Lakew et al. IEEE COMST 2020 doi:10.1109/COMST.2020.2982452; Galkin 2017 arXiv:1710.03701; DATE 2017 ChaCha20-Poly1305 embedded; bearssl.org/constanttime.html

title: Graduate M1 crypto on the Mini ARM-Linux PS independently of the radio PL
rationale: trios-mesh has never run on hardware. M1 crypto (X25519 + ChaCha20-Poly1305) needs only Linux userspace on the Cortex-A9 PS, NOT the AD9361/OFDM PL. Cross-compile to armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf and run smoke-m1 on the Mini as soon as it boots Linux (record in smoke/M1_RESULTS.md) to retire the mesh-software hardware risk in PARALLEL with the high-risk OFDM PL work. README already documents the cross-compile + smoke graduation path. auto=false: requires the physical Mini booting Linux (L0).
board_module: P201 Mini PS (ARM Cortex-A9 Linux) + trios-mesh smoke-m1

title: Port openwifi OFDM PHY to xc7z020 + stage FEC (Viterbi then time-mux LDPC)
rationale: openwifi is a complete open-source 802.11a/g/n OFDM transceiver already synthesizing on adrv9364z7020 (our chip class); measured OFDM Rx = ~15% LUT/~8% DSP on this device. Converts the #1 tech risk from greenfield to a port. Stage FEC: ship convolutional+Viterbi (free with openwifi) first, add a SINGLE time-multiplexed QC-LDPC core only if the host link-budget sim (B07) demands it (parallel LDPC exhausts the 7020). CRITICAL: swap openwifi's Xilinx eval-license Viterbi core (halts after ~2h) before any fielded use. RTL work => auto=false.
board_module: P201/P203 Mini Zynq-7020 PL - P1 radio+mesh

title: External PA+LNA + directional antennas @5.8GHz + DFT-spread-OFDM waveform
rationale: Link math shows no modem/FEC choice saves the link on the onboard 10-15 dBm PA alone (FSPL 108dB@1km/128dB@10km, sensitivity ~-93.8 dBm). External PA (+27..+33 dBm) + LNA + 6 dBi directional antennas gives ~+28 dB margin@1km / ~+8 dB@10km. In free flight the <=100 mW (TH/SG) licensed-by-rule ceiling means directional-antenna GAIN, not raw PA watts, is the legal range lever; full PA on tethered/licensed links. Compound with DFT-spread-OFDM (SC-FDMA) Tx to reclaim the 4-7 dB PAPR back-off. Procurement + waveform RTL => auto=false.
board_module: P201/P203 Mini RF front-end (external) + Tx PHY
citation: internetsociety link-budget; reversepcb FSPL; mathworks SC-FDMA vs OFDM; etrij.15.0114.1194

title: Tether the uplink/anchor node; file BVLOS waiver only for the single free-flyer
rationale: Free-flight endurance (20-45 min) is the #1 HIGH weakness and pure battery physics - not codeable. Tethered UAVs draw continuous ground power (~12 kW/150 m) and achieve 100+ h; AT&T Flying-COW proves tethered LTE 24/7. Make the uplink-hosting node a tethered elevated mast (AX7203 ground role) so only ONE relay flies free. Bonus: a tethered station-keeping anchor is generally NOT a BVLOS operation, so only the single free-flyer needs the ~3-mo CAAS waiver, operating within 5.8 GHz <=100 mW licensed-by-rule. Hardware + paperwork => auto=false.
board_module: topology/anchor (AX7203 tethered ground/mast) + operations/regulatory
citation: beyondsky.xyz tethered-drone; zenithaerotech.com; hologram.io BVLOS; oig.dot.gov FAA BVLOS 2025
